sanskrit: उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम्। ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम्॥
transliteration: uccaiḥśravasam aśvānāṃ viddhi mām amṛtodbhavam | airāvataṃ gajendrāṇāṃ narāṇāṃ ca narādhipam ||
literal_meaning: Among horses I am Uccaiḥśravā, born of the nectar; among lordly elephants I am Airāvata; among humans I am the king.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the apex examples within each category—those beings whose exceptional traits symbolize the divine potential latent in every psyche. By invoking the mythic nectar-born stallion and elephant, plus the ideal ruler, he points Arjuna toward inner sovereignty: the Self that can govern the instinctual ‘herd’ and ‘herd-ego’ with dharmic intelligence.
psychological_state: Heightened aspiration: the ego glimpses its own possibility of enlightened rulership over inner impulses, yet simultaneously confronts the responsibility such sovereignty demands.
trigger_scenario: A mid-level manager is offered a promotion that would make her responsible for 200 people; she feels both exhilarated and terrified of becoming ‘the monarch’ of her own psychic kingdom.
anchor_text: Rule your inner kingdom with the calm power of Airāvata and Uccaiḥśravā.
